description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed primarily from horizontally stacked wooden logs, featuring a gable roof covered with reddish-brown shingles. The cabin sits on a raised stone or concrete foundation and is surrounded by a grassy terrain with a defined pathway leading to the entrance. Tall vertical poles are positioned around the cabin, possibly for structural or decorative purposes. The front facade has a set of double glass doors flanked by windows, while the side view reveals additional windows and a small covered entryway. The overall appearance is robust and traditional, evoking a classic woodland or rural retreat.
description: A traditional log cabin with a gable shingle roof, stone foundation, and prominent vertical poles, featuring double front doors and multiple windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the log construction, vertical poles, and a small covered entryway on the right.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the double glass doors, symmetrical windows, and the main pathway leading to the entrance.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with visible log walls, vertical poles, and the continuation of the pathway.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the gable roof, small windows, and the back of the stone foundation.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, covered in reddish-brown shingles and slight overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple rectangular windows symmetrically placed on the front and sides, with a pair flanking the front doors and additional single windows on the sides; all windows are vertically oriented and evenly spaced.
attribute_door_placement: One set of double doors centered on the front facade beneath the gable, with a small covered entryway visible on the right side.
